topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I managed to click on the subject line: "Scholarship Application Update." The words swam before my eyes—'denied.' A cold, heavy sensation settled in my chest, squeezing the air from my lungs. I reread the rejection letter three times, searching for some misplaced detail, a flaw I could correct, a misunderstanding I could rectify. But there was only the stark finality of the decision. I stood up abruptly, the cheap plastic chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor. My hands started to shake uncontrollably; I needed to leave the room, needed air, needed distance from this crushing disappointment. Every sound in the quiet apartment seemed amplified, mocking my failure.

[story 2]

I stared at the glowing screen, the cursor blinking mockingly on the final paragraph. My stomach twisted into a tight knot, a nauseating lurch that made me lean back in my worn desk chair. The silence of the room was deafening, punctuated only by the frantic drumming of my own pulse in my ears. Why? I replayed every essay, every carefully chosen anecdote, searching for the moment where I had misstepped, the subtle error that invalidated my entire pursuit. A wave of heat rushed up my neck, making my skin prickle. I pressed my palms flat against the cool wood of the desk, trying to anchor myself, but my grip felt weak, inadequate. I squeezed my eyes shut, willing the reality on the page to dissolve, but the stark black text remained, immovable.

[story 3]

When I finally read the notification, the color seemed to drain from the world. It wasn't just the words; it was the sudden, terrifying emptiness that flooded my core. My breath hitched, shallow and rapid, as if my lungs had forgotten how to function correctly. I dropped the phone onto the bedspread, the small sound echoing in the room like a gunshot. I didn't move for a long time, just lay there, feeling utterly unbalanced, as if the floor beneath me had suddenly tilted. A hot flush spread across my cheeks, and I squeezed my eyes shut so tightly that spots danced behind my eyelids. The sheer weight of the outcome pressed down on my shoulders, making it impossible to draw a full breath.
